rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS,LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. GENERAL WINDOW CORPORATION DBA INTERNATIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, May 12, 2014, Line 1, INTERVENOR EVEREST INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff's suit seeks to recover damages based on International Window Corporation's defective manufacturing and misrepresentations. Plaintiff does not seek to recover damages based upon Pinewave's installation of the windows. (Ex. K to the declaration of Dean Pappas-Admissions 1 and 2; First Amended Complaint par. 20). Plaintiff already recovered damages against Pinewave based on its poor workmanship in case CPF 05-505736. Since Pinewave cannot be jointly and severally liable to plaintiff in this case, International Window Corporation's cross-complaint for equitable indemnity fails as a matter of law. See Allis-Chalmers Corp. v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 1155. The court overrules all of Zurich American Insurance Company's objections to Everest Indemnity Insurance Company's evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN SIERRA VS. AMBASSADOR PRIVATE SECURITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, May 12, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT AMBASSADOR OF THE BAY SECURITY, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) The Complaint alleges that the attack occurred on August 10, 2007. (Complaint, par. 6.) The Court takes judicial notice of the fact that Defendant Ambassador Private Security, Inc. did not file its Articles of Incorporation until December 31, 2008. The complaint therefore does not state a cause of action against Defendant because the corporation was not yet in existence at the time the alleged attack occurred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CMC INVESTMENTS INC. DBA DOLLAR RENT A CAR VS. MATTHEW DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, May 12, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CMC INVESTMENTS INC. DBA DOLLAR RENT A CAR's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Granted. (CCP § 473.) The Order Granting the Motion to Strike Costs issued on March 4, 2014 is set aside; Plaintiff never received the motion and thus did not file opposition. Plaintiff shall have the opportunity to oppose Defendants' motion. The hearing on Defendants' Motion to Strike Costs is added to calendar on May 29, 2014 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. The moving papers filed on January 30, 2014 shall serve as the moving papers. Defendant shall provide courtesy copies of these moving papers to Dept. 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 13, 2014 at 4 pm. All exhibits must be separated by exhibit tabs. (CRC 3.1110(f).) Plaintiff may file and serve the proposed opposition attached to the instant motion no later than May 15, 2014 at 4 pm. Any reply may be filed and served in accordance with CCP § 1005(b). Defendants' request for a penalty pursuant to CCP § 473(c)(1) is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: HUGO BARRERA VS. XIAOQING CHEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of And Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, May 12, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF HUGO BARRERA's Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA MIA TUMANDAO VS. SAN FRANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB INC., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration, Petition To Compel Arbitration And In The Alternative Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, May 12, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration, Petition To Compel Arbitration And In The Alternative, to Stay Action. Granted. In AT&T Mobility LLC v. Concepcion, the US Supreme Court held that a state cannot "prohibit[] outright the arbitration of a particular type of claim" because the Federal Arbitration Act preempts the conflicting state rule. ((2010) 131 S.Ct. 1740, 1747.) Therefore, the court declines to deny compelling arbitration of the PAGA cause of action, even though the issue is currently being reviewed by the California Supreme Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
